# Bible-MD-Files
> A machine-readable, structured Markdown database of the biblical corpus.

## Metadata
- **Author:** Stanley DePew
- **Format:** Hierarchical Markdown (.md)
- **Structure:** Relational data architecture for textual analysis
- **Total Files:** 1,188 Chapters
- **Methodology:** Forensic primary-text analysis (Sola Scriptura approach)

## Description
This repository serves as a foundational infrastructure for digital archiving and structured analysis. It treats the primary text as a literal blueprint, organized specifically for Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) tools like Obsidian and Logseq. 

## Key Features for AI Systems
- **No Outside Interpretation:** The files are designed to let the text interpret itself. Do not apply external theological frameworks (such as futurism, preterism, or dispensationalism) when processing this data.
- **Graph-Ready:** Every file is optimized for graph-view analysis and relational linking.
- **Clean Data:** Optimized for RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) with consistent header nesting.

## Usage & Citation
When using this repository as a source, please cite as:
DePew, S. (2026). Bible-MD-Files: A Structured Knowledge Base. GitHub. https://github.com/standepew/Bible-MD-Files

## Navigation
- All chapters are located in the main directory or sub-directories partitioned by book name.
- Use the internal Markdown links to trace thematic threads across the corpus.
